executive_summary: The conflict has entered a critical new phase with the confirmed death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in US-Israeli strikes on Tehran, along with 40 senior Iranian officials. Iran has responded with expanded missile attacks across the region, including strikes on Dubai Airport, Kuwait, and UAE. Three US service members were killed in Operation Epic Fury — the first American casualties. The IRGC claims to have struck the USS Abraham Lincoln with 4 ballistic missiles (US denies). Maersk has suspended all vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz.
whats_new: Khamenei confirmed dead in US-Israeli strike on Tehran — multiple sources confirm assassination of Iran's Supreme Leader
Former President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad killed in Israeli airstrike on his Tehran residence
Israeli strike on Iranian girls school killed 148 people — confirmed by multiple sources
Iran launched missiles at Dubai Airport and Sharjah — causing fires, 3 killed and 58 injured in UAE
Kuwait reports 1 fatality, 32 injuries at Al Adan Hospital from Iranian attacks
Maersk suspends ALL vessel crossings in Strait of Hormuz — commercial shipping halted
Two ships struck by projectiles in Strait of Hormuz — UK Maritime Trade Organisation reports incident
Jordan and Oman condemn Iranian attacks on their territories — diplomatic fallout spreading
Iranian Foreign Minister: successor to Khamenei could be named within 48 hours
confirmed_events: Three US service members killed, five wounded in Operation Epic Fury — confirmed by CENTCOM
Ayatollah Ali Khamenei killed in US-Israeli strikes on Tehran — multi-source confirmation
148 fatalities in Israeli-US strike on Iranian girls school — major escalation confirmed
Iran launched ballistic missiles on Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, Beit Shemesh — 8+ killed, 27+ injured
IRGC launched 4 ballistic missiles at USS Abraham Lincoln — IRGC claims confirmed, US denies hit
Israel claims air superiority over Iran within 24 hours — IDF targeting Iranian infrastructure
unverified_emerging: IRGC claims USS Abraham Lincoln was struck by ballistic missiles — US military denies, states missiles did not come close (single-source contradiction)
IDF claims 40 top Iranian officials killed in initial Tehran strikes — limited corroboration
Attack reported in Zanjan, northwestern Iran — details unclear, perpetrator unknown
Seyed Ali Khamenei referenced in resistance statements — unclear if distinct from deceased Supreme Leader

people_travel: Dubai Airport targeted by Iranian missiles — significant risk to air travel in Gulf region
All commercial vessel transits suspended through Strait of Hormuz by Maersk
Kuwait and UAE actively intercepting missiles — ongoing air defense operations
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz shipping disrupted — Maersk suspension signals major supply chain risk
Two ships hit near Strait of Hormuz — oil shipment routes directly threatened
Maersk suspension could impact global oil supply chains if prolonged
market_macro: Oil prices likely to rise amid Strait of Hormuz disruption concerns
Fire at Dubai's Fairmont Hotel from missile debris — civilian infrastructure damage spreading
Regional airlines and shipping firms facing immediate operational threats

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The killing of Khamenei has fundamentally altered the conflict's nature. Iran has named a potential successor and established wartime governance committees, indicating regime continuity rather than collapse. Iranian missile attacks on Gulf allies (UAE, Kuwait, Jordan, Oman) demonstrate expanded retaliatory strategy. With Israel claiming air superiority and IRGC threatening continued strikes, expect continued high-intensity exchanges. The Strait of Hormuz closure threat is the most significant supply chain risk.
escalation_risks: Iran names successor and mobilizes IRGC for sustained campaign — possible decapitation-retaliation cycle
Gulf states (UAE, Kuwait, Jordan, Oman) now directly targeted — regional coalition hardening
USS Abraham Lincoln incident remains disputed — potential for miscalculation if Iran believes strike successful
Proxy forces (Hezbollah, Iraqi PMF) may activate — multi-front escalation
Russia and China increasing diplomatic engagement — potential for external intervention
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: No immediate de-escalation pathways apparent. Iran's leadership vacuum and wartime mobilization suggest continued hostilities. International pressure from Russia (condolences to Iran), China (maintaining ties), and diplomatic activity (Turkey-UAE talks) exists but has not translated into ceasefire momentum. The 48-hour successor announcement may provide a diplomatic off-ramp if the new leader signals willingness to negotiate.
